item.title: German regulator clamps down on UniCredit ads in Commerzbank takeover bid
item.description: BaFin has prohibited Italian lender from describing German bank as ‘neglected’, ‘unsafe’ or ‘short-term-oriented’

item.title: US stocks race ahead of Europe as Wall Street shrugs off energy shock
item.description: Intel surges above dotcom-era high in latest milestone for tech-powered rebound

item.title: A non-comprehensive list of companies that made less money than Jane Street last year
item.description: hot damn

item.title: BA forced to downgrade thousands more loyalty scheme members after ‘error’
item.description: Some passengers were wrongly told they would keep their Gold or Silver tier status in shake-up of its loyalty programme

item.title: Witkoff and Kushner to travel to Islamabad as hopes rise for Iran peace talks
item.description: US delegation wants to ‘hear Iranians out’ as Tehran’s foreign minister also confirms trip to Pakistan’s capital

item.title: Boots hopes its upcoming IPO will be bigger, bolder, more beautiful
item.description: UK retailer has grown sales at a steady 6% annually over the past five years

item.title: AI data centre emissions vastly underestimated, UK admits
item.description: New projections raise forecasts of climate impact by up to 136 times

item.title: Trading firm Jane Street doubles revenues to $40bn
item.description: Record haul surpasses investment banks which once dominated Wall Street

item.title: Amazon-backed nuclear developer X-energy surges in trading debut
item.description: Company taps rising demand for electricity from data centres to power AI boom

item.title: Private credit won’t spark the next financial crisis
item.description: A lower leverage ratio and limited links to banks mean that the sector is unlikely to pose a systemic threat

item.title: The Apple juggernaut and the AI roadblock
item.description: John Ternus will face innovation challenges that Tim Cook never had to

item.title: London’s luxury hoteliers lose sleep over fall in Middle East visitors
item.description: Expensive rooms go unsold as Iran conflict disrupts Gulf travel and dents demand from big-spending guests

item.title: Stockpickers: Elixirr International, M&C Saatchi, Associated British Foods
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Directors’ Deals: finance chief sells ICA shares ahead of exit
item.description: Group’s share price holds firmer than many of its rivals

item.title: UK Biobank says security checks not imposed because of ‘harms’ to research
item.description: Access to the database has been paused after data breach under pressure from ministers

item.title: Google to invest up to $40bn in Anthropic
item.description: Search giant increases its financial backing to help the AI lab add computing power to run its models

item.title: Investors push for higher yield on $14bn of Oracle-backed data centre debt
item.description: Huge bond offering comes amid concerns over Oracle’s debt load and the recent flood of AI-related issuance

item.title: Judge sanctions woman who sued Leon Black in Epstein-linked case
item.description: Unnamed plaintiff and her former lawyer at Wigdor censured but can continue assault case against billionaire former Apollo chief

item.title: Law firms push staff to return to UAE offices as ceasefire holds
item.description: Some employers will cover costs of returning to Gulf for lawyers who relocated after outbreak of Iran war

item.title: Submit your questions: Who’s afraid of private credit?
item.description: John Foley and Sujeet Indap of the FT’s Lex column will answer your questions live on Thursday April 30 at 1pm BST

item.title: Asian refineries slash output as Iran crisis chokes crude supplies
item.description: Production crunch threatens to worsen regional shortages of jet fuel and diesel

item.title: P&G warns of $1bn hit from Iran war after failing to hedge oil exposure
item.description: US consumer goods group said it may raise prices as conflict drives up costs of fuel and packaging

item.title: Big Four’s shrinkage at the top presages weakness at the bottom line
item.description: Professional service firms appear to be braced for flatlining growth

item.title: Why do I have so much in UK equities?
item.description: British pension schemes will soon be forced to support my portfolio

item.title: How quickly can Gulf oil start gushing again?
item.description: Schrodinger’s Strait

item.title: Lotus unveils plug-in hybrid in latest luxury car pivot away from electric
item.description: British sports-car maker to launch Chinese-made Eletre X in Europe in June

item.title: Collapsed Century Capital lacked approvals, administrators find
item.description: Report into bridging lender finds 2 vehicles did not have anti-money laundering registrations

item.title: Is it time to pave over London’s golf courses?
item.description: Often underused green spaces are being targeted as a creative solution for sclerotic housebuilding rates in the capital. But environmentalists are calling the plan a duff

item.title: White House accuses China of ‘industrial-scale’ theft of AI technology
item.description: Trump official Michael Kratsios says Chinese entities stealing from American labs

item.title: Intel shares set to jump 28% to record high on AI sales boom
item.description: CEO says US chipmaker has made ‘fundamental’ changes after year-long turnaround
